text: Object tracking in video sequences is a key step for robust multi-object tracking (MOT) which, by nature, requires modeling the spatial-temporal interactions between objects. In this work, we design a new tracking framework, named TransMOT, to model the interactions between objects by using graph transformers, and model the object trajectories as a sequence of sparse weighted graphs to handle complex dense scenes. It only needs a spatial graph transformer encoder and a temporal transformer encoder, a spatial graph transformer decoder to deal with the graphs. Compared with the original Transformer model, the proposed model is more efficient and achieves a better tracking performance. To further improve the efficiency and robustness, we propose a cascade association strategy for handling the low-confidence detections and long-term occlusions, which usually require more computations. Experiments are carried out on MOT15, MOT16, MOT17 and MOT20 benchmarks and results show that the proposed TransMOT achieves the state-of-the-art performance on all the four datasets.
